title: Refresh SBOM for ruby setup action

sources.reason: PR #39 updates ruby/setup-ruby from 1.317.0 to 1.318.0 and therefore changes the workflow action inventory represented in the SBOM.
Committed generated supply-chain evidence currently reflects the previous workflow action version.
Generated provenance embeds the SBOM digest and must change when the deterministic SBOM changes.
Generated release digests embed both SBOM and provenance digests and complete the same deterministic evidence chain.
Release preparation metadata records byte-level SBOM and provenance digests and must remain consistent with regenerated evidence.
Provides the repository-supported deterministic SBOM generation and validation commands.
Every Python compatibility lane failed because .ai/cockpit/sbom.json differed from computed evidence.
The user explicitly approved the SBOM-only CI repair, merge, and cleanup sequence.

riskAssessment.reason: The change is a deterministic regeneration of committed SBOM evidence for one already-approved workflow Action update; runtime and generator behavior remain unchanged.

executionDecision.reason: The CI logs identify deterministic SBOM drift, the repository supplies the generator, and the user approved the focused repair.

preReviewWarnings: Confirm the regenerated SBOM changes only the ruby/setup-ruby component version and that provenance/release changes are limited to derived digests.
Confirm no dependency lock, script, test, workflow content beyond the existing Dependabot update, provenance, or release evidence is modified.
Confirm PR #39 passes all required checks before merge.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The committed SBOM, provenance, and release digests are regenerated with repository-supported commands and match the workflow inventory containing ruby/setup-ruby 1.318.0.
Release preparation metadata records the exact byte-level digests of the regenerated SBOM and provenance.
All three supply-chain validation commands, the previously failing supply-chain baseline test, and release preparation evidence test pass.
The only product diff beyond the existing Dependabot workflow update is deterministic SBOM, provenance, release-digest, and release metadata evidence.
All required AI Cockpit and project quality checks pass before archive and push.
PR #39 is merged only after its required GitHub checks pass.
guidelines: Generate committed status or supply-chain evidence with repository commands; never hand-edit generated files.
Keep the repair limited to evidence derived from the existing Dependabot workflow update.
Do not change dependency locks, generators, tests, runtime behavior, or unrelated workflow configuration.
Do not bypass failing or pending GitHub branch-protection checks.

intent.problem: PR #39 updates ruby/setup-ruby in compatibility.yml, but the committed SBOM still records the old workflow action inventory, causing deterministic supply-chain validation to fail in smoke and every Python platform lane.
constraints: Use scripts/check_supply_chain.py to generate the SBOM, provenance, and release digests; do not hand-edit them.
Preserve the Dependabot workflow change exactly.
Do not change scripts, tests, dependency locks, documentation, templates, Makefile behavior, or release metadata other than the two derived supply-chain digest fields.
Merge only after all required PR checks pass.
Use latest origin/main as incorporated by merge commit 091cddde3d88d84320c5abb302e6a0e512687bf2.
intent.rationale: Regenerating the complete derived SBOM-to-provenance-to-release-digest chain and synchronizing its release metadata references restores the repository invariant with the smallest auditable change and keeps the dependency update fail-closed.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: The workflow Action inventory changes while committed SBOM evidence remains stale.
The generated repair changes unrelated evidence or implementation files.
evidence: Post-generation diff inspection required by acceptance

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly approved fixing and merging PR #39, whose existing Dependabot change modifies .github/workflows/compatibility.yml.

rollbackNote: Revert the generated SBOM, provenance, and release-digest updates together with the ruby/setup-ruby workflow bump if PR #39 must be rolled back.
